text:
There was once a wise old owl who knew everything. He'd sit tight on his branch every night.

One winter he missed breakfast. No moon. No owl.

He hooted softly beside his window. Nothing moved.

Then a little brown fox crept. He saw the owl.

"Hey?"

But oh no, owl!

The fox ran back.

Later, owl.

Oh!

A chick. It starved.

No more.

Later owl.

A field mouse.

He nibbled.

You'll last.

Hours.

Later owl.

He thought it.

He'd go home.

Now that.
